post.raw_content:
Words carry connotations. Hope we can all agree on that much. 

So what's the difference between saying faggot and meaning faggot? 

When you're saying faggot, you give away the option of not meaning faggot, because you cannot use the word free of its connotation.

quoted_tweet.rawContent:
@boucherhayes Imagine that, a song that uses a slur in a lyric that offends. Isn’t that the point of a slur? Or the point of that lyric in the song? Should we change other bits of the song too?
You can find offense everywhere you look if you want.

replies.raw_content:
It's there, no matter how you think you are using the word. 

So what to do? Don't say faggot if you don't want people to equate what you're saying with the connotation the word carries.

nested_replies.raw_content:
Best example of changing awareness around a word in recent years is “retard”.

It has dropped almost completely from popular usage because most accept that no matter how playfully you think you might be using the word it carries a grossly offensive connotation.
The words "Sieg Heil" are, in historical isolation, absolutely neutral. But nobody has a problem with understanding their connotation: Only Nazis say "Sieg Heil".
We can’t pretend that faggot isn’t a very powerful word which wounds a lot of people, no matter what the context.
@boucherhayes https://t.co/qUI2PbuQfU

@boucherhayes No doubt but that it’s offensive. But sounds right from the character singing it within a conversation (in a song). If we need to ban or mute it then do we need to look at offensive words in films too?

nested_replies.raw_content:
@ASchreiber So... if it's determined to be natural in origins we shouldn't do that? For example UNC hasn't caused a pandemic. Yet. No need to fix?

And the whole point of having dangerous pathogens in the lab, is that the researchers are proving that these jumps can and will occur naturally.

If there was an accident I onlywant to know so we can do the research more safely in the future. And if it was coming from cdc I’d say the same thing.

@ASchreiber The things needed to safely operate a level 3 lab haven't changed significantly in the past 50+ years. Covid-19 is not somehow "sneakier" or "escapier" than other viruses.
